runs.name: simple_mrt
runs.prompt: my mrt card is not working, can you help me fix it? I need to commute tomorrow and do not have a car

envelope.raw_input: my mrt card is not working, can you help me fix it? I need to commute tomorrow and do not have a car
envelope.normalized_input: The user says their MRT transit card is not working and asks for help fixing it because they must commute tomorrow and do not have a car.
envelope.user_goal: Restore or work around a non-functioning MRT card in time to commute tomorrow.
ambiguity: Exact card failure behavior is not provided.
Card type (stored-value, bank-linked, concession, etc.) is unknown.
No details on whether balance, expiry, or damage checks were already done.
assumptions: User is referring to a public transit fare card used on an MRT system.
User needs actionable help quickly due to next-day commute.
User currently lacks private car access.

plan.objective: Provide direct support to restore or work around a non-functioning MRT card before tomorrow’s commute without any repository, file, command, or runtime side effects.

steps.instruction: Known facts: User reports an MRT transit card is not working, commute is tomorrow, and no car is available. Unknowns: City/operator, card type/issuer, exact failure behavior or error, balance/top-up/expiry status, card condition, and available support channels/hours. Do now: Ask concise clarifying questions on those unknowns and provide immediate safe guidance (basic card checks, lawful backup fare/payment options, and urgent station/help-center escalation steps for next-day travel). Do not do: Do not use tools, files, or commands; do not invent provider-specific rules; do not suggest fare evasion or unsupported fixes. Output: direct_guidance with clarifying questions plus immediate next steps for tomorrow commute readiness.

success_criteria: User receives concise clarifying questions and immediate safe, lawful next steps to restore or work around card access in time for tomorrow’s commute.

runs.name: complex_fintech
runs.prompt: payment dispute processor closes fraud cases when duplicate bank callbacks arrive after manual review lock expires; identify root cause, apply safe fix, include rollback plan, and verify with focused dispute-state checks

envelope.raw_input: payment dispute processor closes fraud cases when duplicate bank callbacks arrive after manual review lock expires; identify root cause, apply safe fix, include rollback plan, and verify with focused dispute-state checks
envelope.normalized_input: Investigate why the payment dispute processor incorrectly closes fraud cases when duplicate bank callbacks arrive after a manual-review lock expires, implement a safe fix, provide a rollback option, and validate with targeted dispute-state checks.
envelope.user_goal: Resolve a concurrency/state-handling defect in dispute processing so fraud cases are not wrongly closed by late duplicate callbacks, with safe remediation and verification.
context_needed: dispute state machine definition and allowed transitions
manual review lock lifecycle and expiration rules
code paths that close fraud cases
event ordering/timing evidence from logs or traces
existing tests covering duplicate callbacks and lock expiry
ambiguity: No repository, language, or service boundaries are provided.
Exact definition of 'manual review lock expires' behavior is unspecified.
Current deduplication strategy for bank callbacks is not described.
Verification environment and test harness are not specified.
Rollback mechanism details (feature flag, deploy rollback, config toggle) are not provided.
assumptions: A software defect exists in dispute-state handling rather than user error.
Duplicate bank callbacks are expected real-world events that should be handled safely.
Fraud-case closure is controlled by application logic in the payment dispute processor.

plan.objective: Identify evidence-based root cause of fraud-case misclosure on duplicate late callbacks, implement a narrowly scoped safe fix, provide rollback capability, and verify dispute-state correctness.

steps.instruction: Known facts: A concurrency/timing defect is reported in payment dispute processing involving duplicate bank callbacks after manual-review lock expiry; rollback and focused verification are required. Unknowns: Exact files, state machine implementation points, dedup keys, lock expiry handlers, and rollback mechanism location. Do now: Discover and map relevant code/tests/config paths for dispute transitions, callback ingestion/idempotency, lock lifecycle, fraud-case closure, and release rollback controls. Do not do: Do not modify files or infer root cause without concrete references. Output: repo_inventory, candidate_paths, state_machine_definition_artifacts, callback_dedup_artifacts, lock_lifecycle_artifacts, closure_path_artifacts, existing_test_inventory, release_rollback_mechanism_artifacts.

Known facts: Candidate implementation and test locations are identified for dispute states, deduplication, lock expiry, and closure paths. Unknowns: Whether evidence fully proves the reported race/order condition and exact failing transition sequence. Do now: Build evidence-backed root-cause analysis linking duplicate/out-of-order callbacks, lock expiry timing, idempotency behavior, and wrongful fraud-case closure transition; record any evidence gaps explicitly. Do not do: Do not propose code mutation yet or assume runtime behavior without traceable code/test/log evidence. Output: root_cause_evidence, event_timing_evidence, analysis_evidence, evidence_gap.

Known facts: Root-cause and timing evidence are available with possible evidence gaps captured. Unknowns: Cross-module dependencies and policy-critical transition guards that could be impacted by a fix. Do now: Identify dependency and policy constraints for dispute-state transitions, idempotency semantics, and fraud-case closure invariants that must hold post-fix. Do not do: Do not design patch logic outside evidenced paths or ignore evidence_gap conditions. Output: dependency_artifacts, policy_transition_constraints, idempotency_invariants.

Known facts: Evidence-backed root cause, timing analysis, dependency mapping, and policy/idempotency invariants are available; rollback readiness is required. Unknowns: None, unless evidence_gap indicates unresolved material uncertainty. Do now: Produce a minimal safe change design that prevents wrongful closure under duplicate/out-of-order callbacks after lock expiry, define narrow mutation_scope, define rollback_plan, and define focused verification_plan tied to dispute-state checks. Do not do: Do not authorize mutation if evidence_gap is material; do not expand scope beyond evidenced paths. Output: fix_design, mutation_scope, rollback_plan, verification_plan, allowed_write_paths, replan_signal.

Known facts: fix_design defines targeted logic changes, mutation_scope and allowed_write_paths bound writable files, rollback_plan exists, and root-cause evidence defines expected correction. Unknowns: None. Do now: Implement only the scoped fix and rollback artifacts consistent with design, preserving or improving idempotency under duplicate/out-of-order callback conditions. Do not do: Do not write outside mutation_scope, do not alter unrelated dispute transitions, and do not claim verification success in this step. Output: change_summary, rollback_patch, updated_test_targets.

Known facts: Scoped code changes and rollback patch are available, with root-cause evidence and verification plan defining expected behavior. Unknowns: Whether all targeted dispute-state and idempotency behaviors now pass without regressions. Do now: Execute focused verification for duplicate callbacks after lock expiry, state-transition policy compliance, idempotency behavior, scope containment, and rollback applicability. Do not do: Do not broaden into unrelated test suites unless required by verification_plan; do not ignore failing focused checks. Output: verification_report, dispute_state_check_results, idempotency_check_results, rollback_validation_result.

Known facts: Verification outputs and change/rollback artifacts are available for final disposition. Unknowns: None. Do now: Produce final implementation summary with root-cause linkage, exact scoped changes, verification outcomes, residual risks, and clear rollback instructions. Do not do: Do not introduce new mutations or unstated claims beyond produced artifacts. Output: final_report.

success_criteria: Root cause is documented with concrete evidence linking lock expiry and duplicate callback handling to wrongful fraud-case closure.
A minimal scoped fix is applied only within mutation_scope with rollback_patch available.
Focused verification confirms policy-compliant dispute-state transitions and safe idempotent behavior under duplicate/out-of-order callbacks.
Rollback procedure is validated and documented in final_report.
